briefing.headline: OBELISCO BLUEPRINTS VANISH!
briefing.report: Carmen lifted a set of theater-district blueprints from a city archive. Witnesses saw her moving along Avenida 9 de Julio beneath a tall white monument.
briefing.callingCard: A torn program from Teatro Colon reads: "Count the lanes, find the needle."

clues: A taxi receipt lists a drop-off at Avenida Corrientes and 9 de Julio.
A witness described a white monument rising from a broad avenue.
A costume tag reads: "Stagehand blacks, Teatro Colon loading door."

puzzle.title: Landmark Deduction
puzzle.description: Use the city clues to identify the Buenos Aires landmark Carmen chose as her rendezvous.
puzzle.question: Which location is Carmen pointing to?
options: The Obelisco on Avenida 9 de Julio
puzzle.visualHtml: 9 de Julio / Corrientes

buenos-aires.funFact: The Obelisco stands at the intersection of Avenida 9 de Julio and Corrientes, two major Buenos Aires landmarks.

briefing.headline: POETRY LEDGER STOLEN!
briefing.report: A ledger of old literary gatherings disappeared from a historic cafe. Carmen left behind a coffee ring and a cryptic note about Avenida de Mayo.
briefing.callingCard: A napkin says: "The oldest table keeps the freshest secret."

clues: The receipt is stamped Avenida de Mayo 825.
A server saw Carmen wearing a poet disguise and carrying a small red notebook.
The calling card smells faintly of strong coffee and medialunas.

puzzle.title: Cafe Trail
puzzle.description: Match the address and cultural clues to the correct Buenos Aires hideout.
puzzle.question: Where should the warrant team go?
puzzle.visualHtml:
☕

Avenida de Mayo 825

buenos-aires.funFact: Cafe Tortoni is one of Buenos Aires most famous historic cafes and has long been associated with writers and artists.

briefing.headline: VENDIMIA CROWN TAKEN!
briefing.report: The ceremonial grape-harvest crown disappeared before a festival rehearsal. Carmen left purple footprints leading toward Mendoza city center.
briefing.callingCard: A grape-stained card reads: "Find me where harvest queens meet the fountains."

clues: A city map has Plaza Independencia circled in purple ink.
A festival sash was found beside a fountain.
A witness saw Carmen dressed as a festival dancer.

puzzle.title: Festival Map Clue
puzzle.description: Use the harvest festival clues to locate Carmen in Mendoza.
puzzle.question: Which hideout matches the evidence?
puzzle.visualHtml:
🍇

Harvest crown + fountain plaza

mendoza.funFact: Mendoza is closely associated with Argentina wine country and the annual grape harvest festival, Vendimia.

briefing.headline: MINERAL MAP MISSING!
briefing.report: A mineral survey map vanished from an Andes research office. Carmen mailed back a card showing a natural stone bridge.
briefing.callingCard: The card says: "Hot springs, cold trail, golden arch."

clues: A route note follows the road toward the Chilean border.
The photo shows a yellow-orange natural bridge over a river.
A borrowed jacket is labeled "geology field assistant."

puzzle.title: Andes Landmark Match
puzzle.description: Read the mountain clues and identify the correct Mendoza hideout.
puzzle.question: Which landmark is Carmen using?
puzzle.visualHtml: Golden bridge / Andes route

mendoza.funFact: Puente del Inca is known for its naturally formed mineral-colored bridge in the Andes.

briefing.headline: FOLK GUITAR STOLEN!
briefing.report: A historic peña guitar disappeared after a night of folk music. Tire tracks point south through red-rock valleys.
briefing.callingCard: A lyric scrap reads: "Follow the echo through the quebrada to the wine town."

clues: A bus ticket is marked Salta to Cafayate.
A witness heard a zamba tune near red sandstone cliffs.
Carmen bought a poncho and posed as a folk singer.

puzzle.title: Valley Route
puzzle.description: Use music and landscape clues to identify Carmen trail south of Salta.
puzzle.question: Where is the hideout?
puzzle.visualHtml:
🎸

Red valley / folk song / wine town

salta.funFact: Cafayate is known for dramatic valley landscapes and high-altitude wine production.

briefing.headline: COLONIAL SEAL SWIPED!
briefing.report: A colonial seal vanished from an old civic archive. Carmen left a sketch of arches facing Salta main square.
briefing.callingCard: The card says: "Under the arcade, history keeps quiet."

clues: The clue sketch shows a long colonial arcade.
A plaza vendor saw Carmen wearing an archivist badge.
The route never leaves Salta city center.

puzzle.title: Colonial Architecture Clue
puzzle.description: Match the arcade and city-center clues to the Salta location.
puzzle.question: Which hideout should ACME search?
puzzle.visualHtml:
🏛️

Arcades facing the main square

salta.funFact: Salta preserves notable colonial architecture around its central square.

briefing.headline: UNIVERSITY KEY DISAPPEARS!
briefing.report: An old ceremonial key vanished from the university quarter. Carmen left notes about courtyards, churches, and early classrooms.
briefing.callingCard: A stamped card reads: "The block remembers every lesson."

clues: A campus guide has the Jesuit Block underlined.
A witness saw Carmen in a professor robe.
The clue mentions historic classrooms near the city center.

puzzle.title: Heritage Deduction
puzzle.description: Use the university and heritage clues to find Carmen hideout.
puzzle.question: Which Córdoba location fits?
puzzle.visualHtml:
📚

Old classrooms / city center

cordoba.funFact: Córdoba has one of the oldest university traditions in the Americas.

briefing.headline: STAR CHART STOLEN!
briefing.report: A night-sky chart was stolen before a public astronomy talk. Carmen left a mountain road toll receipt.
briefing.callingCard: A black card reads: "Count the stars from the sierras."

clues: The car receipt points west toward the Sierras de Córdoba.
A telescope lens cap was found in Carmen red coat pocket.
A witness saw her dressed as a night guide.

puzzle.title: Mountain Night Clue
puzzle.description: Follow the star-chart and mountain-road evidence.
puzzle.question: Where is Carmen hiding?
puzzle.visualHtml:
✦

Star chart / mountain road

cordoba.funFact: The Sierras de Córdoba are a defining landscape around the province.

briefing.headline: JUNGLE FIELD NOTES LIFTED!
briefing.report: A ranger notebook vanished from the park office. Carmen left damp footprints heading to a lower waterfall trail.
briefing.callingCard: A wet card reads: "Look below the mist."

clues: A trail pass is stamped Circuito Inferior.
The clue mentions spray rising from below the walkways.
Carmen borrowed a biologist vest.

puzzle.title: Waterfall Circuit
puzzle.description: Use the park trail clues to identify the circuit.
puzzle.question: Which hideout matches the evidence?
puzzle.visualHtml:
🌿

Below the mist / ranger trail

iguazu.funFact: Iguazú National Park has multiple walkways that reveal the falls from different levels.

briefing.headline: BORDER COMPASS STOLEN!
briefing.report: A compass used for geography lessons disappeared. Carmen left three colored ribbons: Argentina, Brazil, and Paraguay.
briefing.callingCard: A triangular card reads: "Three countries, one river meeting."

clues: A tourist map marks the Triple Frontier viewpoint.
The ribbons match three national colors.
A witness saw Carmen disguised as a geography teacher.

puzzle.title: Border Deduction
puzzle.description: Use the river and three-country clues.
puzzle.question: Where is Carmen heading?
puzzle.visualHtml:
△

Three borders / river meeting

iguazu.funFact: Puerto Iguazú sits near the border area shared by Argentina, Brazil, and Paraguay.

briefing.headline: LAKE CHART STOLEN!
briefing.report: A historic navigation chart vanished from a lakeside exhibit. Carmen left a chocolate wrapper folded into a boat.
briefing.callingCard: A blue card reads: "The lake reflects the route."

clues: The wrapper came from a chocolate shop on Mitre Street.
A boat schedule points across Nahuel Huapi Lake.
Carmen purchased a captain cap.

puzzle.title: Lake Route
puzzle.description: Use the boat and chocolate clues to locate the Bariloche hideout.
puzzle.question: Where should ACME search?
puzzle.visualHtml:
⛵

Lake chart / chocolate wrapper

bariloche.funFact: Nahuel Huapi Lake is central to Bariloche scenery and tourism.

briefing.headline: ALPINE CLOCK FACE MISSING!
briefing.report: A decorative clock face disappeared from a civic exhibit. Carmen left boot prints in the town square.
briefing.callingCard: A square card says: "Stone walls, timber lines, civic time."

clues: The photo shows alpine-style stone and wood buildings.
A witness saw Carmen dressed as a municipal guide.
The trail stays in central Bariloche, not the ski slopes.

puzzle.title: Architecture Match
puzzle.question: Which Bariloche hideout fits?
puzzle.visualHtml:
■

Stone square / timber lines

bariloche.funFact: Bariloche civic architecture is known for its alpine-inspired stone and timber character.

briefing.headline: SHIP LOG BOOK STOLEN!
briefing.report: A Beagle Channel ship log vanished before morning departure. Carmen left a red scarf tied to a pier cleat.
briefing.callingCard: A salt-stained card reads: "Follow the channel at the end of the world."

clues: The manifest lists a small boat crossing the Beagle Channel.
A witness saw Carmen wearing a sailor coat.
The route points away from the airport and toward the harbor.

puzzle.title: Harbor Trail
puzzle.description: Use the maritime clues to identify the Ushuaia hideout.
puzzle.question: Where is Carmen going?
puzzle.visualHtml:
⚓

Ship log / harbor cleat

ushuaia.funFact: The Beagle Channel is one of Ushuaia most recognizable maritime settings.

briefing.headline: POSTMARK PLATE PILFERED!
briefing.report: A souvenir postmark plate disappeared from a remote tourism desk. Carmen left a postcard marked "Fin del Mundo."
briefing.callingCard: The card reads: "Send the warrant to the last post."

clues: The clue points to end-of-the-world tourism routes.
A stack of postcards is tied with red thread.
Carmen wore a postal clerk disguise.

puzzle.title: End-of-World Clue
puzzle.description: Use the postcard clues to identify the hideout.
puzzle.question: Where should the warrant team search?
options: End of the World post office
puzzle.visualHtml:
✉

Fin del Mundo

warrantAnswers.hideout: End of the World post office

ushuaia.funFact: Ushuaia is strongly associated with the phrase Fin del Mundo, or End of the World.

briefing.headline: ICE AGE DIAGRAM TAKEN!
briefing.report: A glacier formation diagram disappeared from a science exhibit. Carmen left a frosted envelope stamped Glaciarium.
briefing.callingCard: A cold card reads: "Learn the ice before you cross it."

clues: A museum ticket is stamped Glaciarium.
The note references glacier science, not a boat dock.
Carmen wore a museum educator badge.

puzzle.title: Science Exhibit Clue
puzzle.description: Use the museum and glacier-science clues.
puzzle.question: Where is Carmen hiding?
puzzle.visualHtml:
❄

Science exhibit / frost envelope

el-calafate.funFact: El Calafate is a gateway for visitors learning about and visiting Patagonian glaciers.

briefing.headline: PATAGONIAN ROUTE MAP GONE!
briefing.report: A route map for glacier boats vanished from a travel office. Carmen left a blue ticket stub beside a windblown berry branch.
briefing.callingCard: A card reads: "Where the lake carries ice, I carry the loot."

clues: The boat ticket references Lago Argentino.
The clue mentions ice floating on a large Patagonian lake.
Carmen borrowed a deckhand coat.

puzzle.title: Lake and Ice Trail
puzzle.description: Use the boat-route clues around El Calafate.
puzzle.question: Which hideout fits the evidence?
puzzle.visualHtml:
▰

Lake route / floating ice

el-calafate.funFact: Lago Argentino is the large lake associated with glacier routes near El Calafate.
